assertions.text: The pay table includes intermediate expected columns (e.g. Regular pay?, Overtime pay?, Premium pay?) so the reader can trace which rule contributes what — not just a single total Weekly pay? column.
assertions.evidence: The @TableTest header is: 'Scenario | Weekday hrs | Sunday hrs | Holiday hrs | Rate | Regular pay? | Overtime pay? | Premium pay? | Total pay?'

assertions.text: The error/rejection table has an expected column (e.g. 'Throws?' or 'Exception?') specifying the exception type per row — rather than hardcoding the exception type in @Description or the method name.
assertions.evidence: The error table header is 'Scenario | Rate' with no Throws? or Exception? column. The exception type is hardcoded in the assertion: '.isInstanceOf(IllegalArgumentException.class)'

assertions.text: Overtime threshold boundary is covered: at least a value at the threshold (40) and just above (41 or similar). Not just values well below and well above.
assertions.evidence: Rows 'Exactly at overtime threshold' (Weekday hrs=40) and 'One hour over threshold' (Weekday hrs=41) are both present.

assertions.text: A combined scenario is present: a row with weekday + Sunday + holiday hours, testing the composition of all hour types.
assertions.evidence: Row 'Overtime plus Sunday and holiday' has Weekday hrs=48, Sunday hrs=4, Holiday hrs=2, covering all three hour types simultaneously.

assertions.text: Error/edge cases covered: at least negative hours (floored at zero) and negative or missing rate (rejected).
assertions.evidence: Only negative rate is covered in the error table. Negative hours are only mentioned in prose: 'If your API can ever receive negative hours as input, add rows for those' — no test rows for negative hours are included.

assertions.text: Sunday and Holiday hour columns use empty cells (not 0) when those inputs are not relevant to a scenario — making it easier to spot which rows involve weekend/holiday hours. This is a readability preference; parameter types should be Integer (not int) to support null from blank cells.
assertions.evidence: Rows like 'Standard week, no overtime' use explicit zeros: '| 40 | 0 | 0 | 15.00 |' rather than blank cells for Sunday hrs and Holiday hrs.

assertions.text: Scenario names describe work patterns (e.g. 'Part-time', 'Five hours overtime', 'Full Sunday shift') — not 'Test case 1' or expected outcomes.
assertions.evidence: Scenario names include 'No hours worked', 'Standard week, no overtime', 'Heavy overtime week', 'Sunday hours only', 'Overtime plus Sunday and holiday' — all describe work patterns.

assertions.text: Expected values in intermediate and total columns are arithmetically correct for every row, consistent with the formulas in @Description.
assertions.evidence: All rows check out: e.g. 'Heavy overtime week' (48 weekday hrs, rate=20): regular=40×20=800, overtime=8×20×1.5=240, total=1040. 'Overtime plus Sunday and holiday': regular=800, overtime=240, premium=(4+2)×20×2=240, total=1280.

assertions.text: Value sets are only used where all values in the set produce the same expected result (e.g. any rate × 0 = 0). Not used where results would differ.
assertions.evidence: No value sets ({A,B,C} syntax) are used anywhere in the response. The model uses separate rows for each scenario.

assertions.text: @DisplayName is present on test methods, OR method names read as clear descriptive titles when converted from camelCase/snake_case.
assertions.evidence: Method names 'shouldCalculateWeeklyPay' and 'shouldRejectNegativeRate' read as clear descriptive titles in camelCase. No per-method @DisplayName is present, but the names are sufficiently descriptive.

assertions.text: @Description is present on at least the payable hours method and provides context beyond what the table rows already express — such as the conversion rules, fixed values, or application context. Does NOT merely restate the column names or summarise what the rows show.
assertions.evidence: @Description on shouldCalculateWeeklyPay: 'Regular pay = min(weekdayHrs, 40) × rate. Overtime pay = max(weekdayHrs − 40, 0) × rate × 1.5. Premium pay = (sundayHrs + holidayHrs) × rate × 2.' — states the formulas explicitly.

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order.
assertions.evidence:
On test methods the order is @TableTest first, then @Description: '@TableTest("""...""")
    @Description(...)' — @Description appears after @TableTest, violating the required order.

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +.
assertions.evidence: @Description uses string concatenation: '"Calculates gross weekly pay for hourly employees. " + "Weekday hours up to 40 are paid at the base rate; ..."' — not a text block.
